title: Boston · Forty Years of Crime
subtitle: FBI UCR 1985–2015 + BPD records 2015–2026 · 12 districts, resident names · real locations

hook.line: Boston's reported crime since its 1989 peak
hook.sub: FBI UCR 1985–2015 · fully sourced, mapped to this week

punchline.sub: −71% under the same FBI measure · crime-only reports fell another 36% by 2025

copy.chapter2Kicker: CHAPTER 2 · THE LAST 5 YEARS · BPD / ANALYZE BOSTON
copy.chapter2Caption: Monthly reports, 2021–2026. Every dot is a real reported incident location (block-level, sampled).
copy.transitionDesc: The chart counted per year. From here the map counts per month across 12 districts — every dot a real reported location, anonymized to the block.
copy.methodRecentSub: the last five years · month by month · real locations
copy.methodDotsSub: Every dot is a real reported incident location. 99.4% map to a district. BPD's file mixes crime and service records — the trend counts crime only.
copy.methodFootnote: FBI UCR (through 2015) and BPD records (2015+) differ; BPD's public file excludes sexual-assault records — disclosed, not zero.
copy.quizQuestion: Which Boston district is the safest?
copy.seamExplain: The ruler changes here — not the city. Through 2015 the gray bars are the FBI's yearly tally of Boston's classic “index” crimes — murder, robbery, assault, burglary, theft. In August 2015 BPD switched to a new incident records system, so 2016 begins a different, case-by-case count under a broader taxonomy — a change of source and measure, not a real jump in crime. Compare the shape within one color, never across the dashed line.
copy.sourceLine: Data: Boston Police Department via Analyze Boston (public domain) · sexual-assault records excluded by BPD
copy.coverageText: 99.4% of records mapped to a district
copy.creditsSources: Sources: BPD incident reports (2015–present, ODC-PDDL public domain) via Analyze Boston & FBI UCR (1985–2015) via the FBI Crime Data Explorer. District names per boston.gov. BPD's public file excludes sexual-assault records for privacy — a gap, not zero. Basemap: © OpenStreetMap contributors (ODbL).

historyNotes.text: 1989: the peak — 70,003 crimes in one year.
The long fall accelerates through the '90s.
2015: 20,110 — down 71% from the peak; the modern record begins.
Crime-only reports keep falling — 36% below 2016 by 2025.

annotations.text: Every dot is a real reported incident location — BPD via Analyze Boston. Sexual-assault records are excluded from the public file (a gap, not zero).
South End (District D-4) — Boston's busiest for reported crime, and rising: +33% since 2022.
Charlestown — the quietest district, down 18% since 2022.
October 2025 — the busiest month of the last five years: 7,398 reports.
